github.com/andy-esch/taskflow/internal/c: AuditShowEnvelope is `audit show --json`.
AuditsEnvelope is `audit list --json`.
Column is one projectable column of a list table: a machine name (the header in `-o table`, and what `-c/--columns` selects), a short description (for completion), and an extractor.
ColumnSpec is the name+description of a column without the (typed) extractor, so the cli completion/help layer can offer and describe columns without the generic type parameter.
CreatedEnvelope is `task/epic/audit new --json`.
CreatedItem is the created document inside CreatedEnvelope.
EpicShowEnvelope is `epic show --json`.
EpicsEnvelope is `epic list --json`.
ErrorEnvelope is the failure payload emitted under --json (see cli.WriteError).
ErrorItem is the error body inside ErrorEnvelope.
FindingsEnvelope is `audit findings --json` (the finding-level query).
FixEnvelope is `lint --fix --json`.
InitEnvelope is `init --json`.
KindSchema is the per-kind authoring guidance (`tskflwctl schema <kind>`): how to compose a well-formed document of that kind.
LintEnvelope is `lint --json` and `audit lint --json` (the same per-entity slug+issues shape backs both).
MoveResult is the per-item outcome of a transition.
MovesEnvelope is the transition report (`task start --json`, etc.).
SchemaContract is the global machine contract (`tskflwctl schema`): everything an agent needs to drive the tool without parsing --help prose.
SchemaEnvelope is `schema --json` (the global contract).
SchemaExitCode is one exit code and its stable machine name (also the `code` in the --json error envelope).
SchemaField is one known frontmatter field and its YAML storage type.
SchemaKindEnvelope is `schema <kind> --json` (per-kind authoring guidance).
SchemaStatus is one task status and whether it is part of the working set.
Style renders optional ANSI styling and carries the output width (0 = no limit).
SummaryEnvelope is `status --json`.
TaskMutationEnvelope is `task set` / `task append` / `task set --body` under --json: the reloaded task, dry_run, and (for the body commands) the resulting body.
TaskShowEnvelope is `task show --json`.
TasksEnvelope is `task list --json`.
TemplateInfo is one body template's listable metadata (kind/name/description), populated by the cli for `template list`/`show`.
TemplateShowEnvelope is `template show --json` (a template's metadata + body).
TemplatesEnvelope is `template list --json`.
VersionEnvelope is `version --json`.
github.com/andy-esch/taskflow/internal/d: Audit is a code-audit document.
AuditBucket is an audit's lifecycle state, identical to the directory it lives in (audits/<bucket>/).
Descriptor is the per-entity metadata the tool would otherwise hand-enumerate in a `switch kind` at every layer.
frontmatter a drafter fills in (not tool-managed stamps)
short, factual "how to write it" rules
top-level planning dir (TasksDir / EpicsDir / AuditsDir)
the `schema <kind>` word: task | epic | audit
body scaffolds offered for this kind; the one named DefaultTemplate is used when --template is omitted
Epic is a long-lived domain goal.
FieldDoc documents one *authoring* field: the human/agent-set frontmatter a well-formed document declares.
FileProblem is a non-fatal per-file load problem: the file was skipped, with an actionable message explaining what's wrong and how to fix it.
Finding is one parsed audit finding.
XS | S | M | L
open | in-progress | fixed | landed | deferred | superseded | wontfix (see FindingStatuses)
acute | soon | eventually
FixResult records the auto-repairs applied (or proposed) for one file.
Issue is a single frontmatter lint finding.
NamedTemplate is one body scaffold a kind offers under a name.
Status is a task lifecycle state.
Task is a planning task.

Declared is the status the frontmatter literally claimed. Status is the
authoritative one (its directory); the two diverge only when a file is
misfiled — see Misfiled.
stamped when a task enters in-progress (incl. `new --start`)
UnsetField is a sentinel value in a SetFields update map: the key is removed from the frontmatter instead of being assigned.
